# S.E.E. Official Library

> Every authoring guide for the S.E.E. Official Library, combined on one page. Learn how to create workflows, prompts, skills, commands, and bundles and publish them to the catalog.

## Contents

- Bundles
- Commands
- Authoring guide
- Prompts
- Publish to the catalog
- Skills
- Workflows
- Bundle schema
- Command schema
- Catalog schema
- Prompt schema
- Rule &amp; template schema
- Skill schema
- Workflow schema

---

# Bundles

Source: content/authoring/bundles.mdx

A **bundle** package installs several packages together as one unit - for
example a workflow plus the prompt, skill, and command it references.

- Category: `bundle`
- Payload: none of its own; a bundle points at other packages' payloads
- Metadata: `s_e_e_package.json`

## Metadata shape

A bundle's `s_e_e_package.json`:

- `id` - bundle id (e.g. `bundle-author-story`).
- `slug` - url/file-safe slug.
- `category` - `bundle`.
- `name` - display name.
- `description` - one-line summary.
- `labels` - string array (deduped and sorted by the build).
- `dependencies` - string array of other package ids.

Reference example (`packages/author-story/s_e_e_package.json`):

```json
{
  "id": "bundle-author-story",
  "slug": "author-story",
  "category": "bundle",
  "name": "Author story from description",
  "description": "Packages linked by template references: workflows, prompts, skills, commands.",
  "labels": ["story"],
  "dependencies": []
}
```

## The `files[]` install map

The bundle's `catalog.json` entry carries a `files[]` array - one `{ to, from }`
per installed file. `from` is the source payload path in this repo
(`packages/{slug}/{version}/{file}`); `to` is where it lands on the install
target.

```json
{
  "files": [
    { "to": ".agents/skills/stories/SKILL.md", "from": "packages/author-story/1.0.0/stories/SKILL.md" },
    { "to": ".s_e_e/commands/cursor-agent.json", "from": "packages/author-story/1.0.0/cursor-agent/command.json" },
    { "to": ".s_e_e/workflows/definitions/system-author-story.json", "from": "packages/author-story/1.0.0/system-author-story/definition.json" }
  ]
}
```

For a bundle, the validator infers each file's kind from its `to` path, so the
`to` prefix must be namespaced correctly:

- `.s_e_e/workflows/definitions/…` → workflow
- `.s_e_e/prompts/…` → prompt
- `.s_e_e/commands/…` → command
- `.agents/skills/…/SKILL.md` → skill
- `*.mdc` → rule
- `templates/…` → template

## `to`-path rule

Every install path must start with one of `.s_e_e/`, `.agents/`, `.cursor/`, or
`templates/`, use forward slashes, and be unique within the package.

## Schema reference

`see.library/v1` - see the [bundle schema](/schema/bundle) and the
[catalog schema](/schema/).

---

# Commands

Source: content/authoring/commands.mdx

A **command** package is an agent-runner definition: it maps typed inputs to a
CLI binary invocation. Workflows reference a command by `id` from a `command`
task.

- Category: `command`
- Payload: a single `command.json`
- Installs to: `.s_e_e/commands/{id}.json` (the install path must be exactly this)

## File shape

Key fields:

- `id` - unique command id; also the install filename. Required, non-empty.
- `name`, `icon` - display metadata.
- `binary` - the executable to run. Required, non-empty.
- `base_args` - args always passed to the binary.
- `forced_args` - args the runner appends and the operator cannot override.
- `workspace_arg` - how the working directory is passed (`flag` + templated `value`).
- `prompt` - how the prompt is delivered (e.g. `{ "delivery": "stdin" }`).
- `params` - typed parameters, each with a `map.target` describing where the
  value goes (`env`, `timeout`, `args_append`, `lock_policy`, `stream_toggle`).
- `env` - static environment variables.
- `result` - success condition and captured fields.
- `execution` - cwd (`spoke`/`hub`), lock scope/policy, streaming, interactivity.

## Reference example

From `packages/cursor-agent/1.0.0/command.json`:

```json
{
  "id": "cursor-agent",
  "name": "Cursor Agent",
  "icon": "cursor",
  "binary": "cursor",
  "base_args": ["agent", "-p", "--trust", "--force"],
  "forced_args": ["--output-format", "stream-json", "--stream-partial-output"],
  "workspace_arg": { "flag": "--workspace", "value": "{{workspace}}" },
  "prompt": { "delivery": "stdin" },
  "params": [
    {
      "key": "cursor_model",
      "type": "string",
      "default": "composer-2.5",
      "map": { "target": "env", "name": "CURSOR_MODEL" }
    },
    {
      "key": "timeout_secs",
      "type": "number",
      "default": 1800,
      "map": { "target": "timeout" }
    }
  ],
  "env": { "CURSOR_NON_INTERACTIVE": "1", "GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT": "0" },
  "result": {
    "success_when": "exit_code == 0",
    "capture": ["run_id", "exit_code", "duration_ms", "stdout_tail", "stderr_tail", "truncated"]
  },
  "execution": {
    "cwd": "spoke",
    "lock": { "scope": "spoke", "default_policy": "force" },
    "stream": { "format": "stream-json" },
    "non_interactive": true
  }
}
```

## Rules

- The catalog validator requires `command` payloads to install under
  `.s_e_e/commands/`, and the path must equal `.s_e_e/commands/{id}.json`.
- A payload with a `content` field but no `binary` is treated as a prompt, not a
  command.

## Schema reference

`see.library/v1` - see the [command schema](/schema/command) and the
[catalog schema](/schema/).

---

# Authoring guide

Source: content/authoring/index.mdx

How to create each S.E.E. library piece and publish it to the catalog.

A **package** is a versioned payload plus a manifest entry. Every package has a
`category` that decides its file shape and where it installs on a target
(`.s_e_e/`, `.agents/`, `.cursor/`, or `templates/`).

## Pieces

- [Commands](/authoring/commands) - agent runner definitions that map inputs to a CLI binary.
- [Prompts](/authoring/prompts) - reusable system prompts referenced by workflows and commands.
- [Skills](/authoring/skills) - `SKILL.md` guides that teach an agent a repeatable capability.
- [Workflows](/authoring/workflows) - multi-step task definitions the engine runs against a spoke.
- [Bundles](/authoring/bundles) - curated sets of packages installed together.

## Publishing

- [Publish to the catalog](/authoring/publish) - payload layout, the `catalog.json` entry, and the build/validate scripts.

## Schema reference

The manifest schema is `see.library/v1`. See the [catalog schema](/schema/) for
the authoritative contract, and the per-type payload schemas linked from each
guide above.

---

# Prompts

Source: content/authoring/prompts.mdx

A **prompt** package is a reusable system prompt. Workflows inject it with the
`{{prompt.ID}}` template placeholder; commands can deliver it to a binary.

- Category: `prompt`
- Payload: a single `prompt.json`
- Installs to: `.s_e_e/prompts/{file}.json`

## File shape

- `id` - prompt id used by `{{prompt.ID}}`. Required, non-empty. May carry a
  namespace prefix (e.g. `system-story-authoring`).
- `name` - display name. Required, non-empty.
- `content` - the prompt body (a single string; use `\n` for line breaks).
- `created_at` - ISO-8601 timestamp (optional).

A payload that contains `tasks` is treated as a workflow, not a prompt.

## Reference example

Abbreviated from `packages/system-story-authoring/1.0.0/prompt.json`:

```json
{
  "id": "system-story-authoring",
  "name": "Author story",
  "content": "You are authoring a new S.E.E. story for the backlog board.\n\nGoals:\n- Turn the operator request into one atomic story markdown file under `.s_e_e/stories/stories/`.\n...",
  "created_at": "2026-07-04T11:34:01.585966809Z"
}
```

## Guidance

- Keep the prompt self-contained: name the deliverable, the constraints, and the
  references the agent should read.
- Reference the prompt from a workflow `command` task via
  `{{prompt.system-story-authoring}}`; the id must exist in the hub prompt store
  when the workflow runs (installing the bundle satisfies this).

## Schema reference

`see.library/v1` - see the [prompt schema](/schema/prompt) and the
[catalog schema](/schema/).

---

# Publish to the catalog

Source: content/authoring/publish.mdx

Adding a package is two parts: the payload files under `packages/`, and a
manifest entry in `catalog.json`. Then run the build to validate and refresh the
manifest.

## 1. Payload layout

Place the payload under a versioned directory:

```
packages/{slug}/{version}/{file}
```

For a bundle, also add `packages/{slug}/s_e_e_package.json` metadata (when a slug
has multiple entries, the file is `s_e_e_package.{id}.json`).

## 2. Catalog entry

Append an entry to `catalog.json` `packages[]`. Required fields:

- `id` - globally unique within the source.
- `slug` - url/file-safe.
- `category` - `workflow` | `prompt` | `skill` | `rule` | `template` | `command` | `bundle`.
- `version` - semver.
- `author` - string.
- `verified` - bool.
- `license` - SPDX id (e.g. `AGPL-3.0-only`).
- `requires` - string array of external tools (empty when none).
- `dependencies` - string array of other package ids.
- `files[]` - install map of `{ to, from }`.

```json
{
  "id": "prompt-system-story-authoring",
  "slug": "system-story-authoring",
  "category": "prompt",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "author": "garunski",
  "verified": true,
  "license": "AGPL-3.0-only",
  "requires": [],
  "dependencies": [],
  "files": [
    {
      "to": ".s_e_e/prompts/system-story-authoring.json",
      "from": "packages/system-story-authoring/1.0.0/prompt.json"
    }
  ]
}
```

Every `to` path must start with `.s_e_e/`, `.agents/`, `.cursor/`, or
`templates/`.

## 3. Build and validate

```bash
npm run build      # validate payloads and refresh catalog.json `updated`
npm run validate   # build-catalog.mjs --check: no writes, fails if invalid or stale
```

`npm run validate` is what CI runs. It fails if the catalog is invalid or out of
sync with `packages/`, so run `npm run build` and commit both the payload and
the updated `catalog.json`.

## Schema reference

`see.library/v1` - see the [catalog schema](/schema/).

---

# Skills

Source: content/authoring/skills.mdx

A **skill** package is an agent skill: a `SKILL.md` file with YAML frontmatter
and a markdown body that teaches an agent a repeatable capability.

- Category: `skill`
- Payload: a `SKILL.md` file
- Installs to: `.agents/skills/{slug}/SKILL.md`

## File shape

The payload must be a `SKILL.md` that starts with YAML frontmatter. The
validator requires:

- `name` - non-empty skill name.
- `description` - non-empty; the activation description an agent uses to decide
  when to load the skill.

The markdown body after the frontmatter is the skill instructions.

## Reference example

Frontmatter from `packages/workflow/1.0.0/SKILL.md`:

```markdown
---
name: workflow
description: >-
  Authors and edits S.E.E. workflow JSON definitions stored under the hub
  `.s_e_e/workflows/definitions/`. Validates against `workflow.schema.json`, the
  engine task handlers (cli_command, command, user_input, git_action,
  story_action, foreach, custom), and template expansion. Activates on
  `/workflow`, requests to "create a workflow", or edits to workflow JSON.
---

# S.E.E. workflow authoring

Authors and edits workflow JSON for the S.E.E. engine...
```

## Guidance

- Write the `description` as activation triggers: name the phrases and file
  types that should load the skill. This is what an agent matches against.
- Keep the body task-focused: MUST/NEVER rules, a minimal working example, and
  an authoring checklist.

## Schema reference

`see.library/v1` - see the [skill schema](/schema/skill) and the
[catalog schema](/schema/).

---

# Workflows

Source: content/authoring/workflows.mdx

A **workflow** package is a JSON definition the S.E.E. engine executes as a tree
of tasks against a spoke. It can drive agents, run shell commands, collect
operator input, and mutate the stories/knowledge stores.

- Category: `workflow`
- Payload: a single `definition.json`
- Installs to: `.s_e_e/workflows/definitions/{slug}.json`

## File shape

An outer envelope wraps the engine `content`:

- `id` - `system-<name>` for defaults, `user-<name>` for user-created.
- `name`, `description`, `version`.
- `content` - the engine workflow.

The engine `content` requires `id`, `name`, and a non-empty `tasks` array, and
optionally declares `runtime_inputs` and root paths (`spoke_root`, `hub_root`,
`stories_root`, `knowledge_root`).

Each task has a unique `id`, a `name`, a single-handler `function`, and optional
nested `next_tasks` (depth-first execution). Optional fields: `capture_output`,
`wait_for`, `continue_on_failure`, and `run_when` (conditional execution on a
prior task's captured output).

## Task handlers

- `cli_command` - deterministic shell (`input.command`, `input.args`).
- `command` - run an installed command by `input.command_id`, with optional
  `input.prompt` and `input.config`.
- `user_input` - pause for operator input; later tasks read it via
  `{{userinput.TASK_ID.value}}`.
- `git_action` - git operation.
- `story_action` - stories/knowledge read or mutation.
- `foreach` - loop over a string array (`input.item_runtime_key` + a collection
  source).
- `custom` - escape hatch.

A `function` object must contain exactly one handler key.

## Template expansion

- `{{prompt.ID}}` - injects a stored prompt; the id must exist in the hub.
- `{{runtime.KEY}}` - a run-start value; every key must be declared in
  `runtime_inputs` and must not contain dots.
- `{{userinput.TASK_ID.value}}` - a value from an earlier `user_input` task.
- `{{task.TASK_ID.output...}}` - captured output from a task with
  `capture_output: true` (`cli_command` yields `{ exit_code, stdout, stderr }`).

## Conditional tasks

- `run_when` - run only when a comparison on a producer task's captured output is
  true. Producer must set `capture_output: true`. Ops: `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `lt`,
  `ge`, `le`, `exists`.
- `continue_on_failure` - when a producer may fail but descendants should still
  run (pair with `run_when` on `exit_code` for fix-on-fail patterns).
- `wait_for` - block until listed task ids (or all iterations of a foreach id)
  complete.

## Reference example

Minimal valid workflow (from the `workflow` skill):

```json
{
  "id": "system-hello-workflow",
  "name": "Hello workflow",
  "description": "Smallest valid workflow.",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "content": {
    "id": "hello-workflow",
    "name": "Hello workflow",
    "spoke_root": "{{runtime.spoke_root}}",
    "runtime_inputs": [
      { "key": "spoke_root", "input_type": "string", "required": true, "label": "Spoke repo root" }
    ],
    "tasks": [
      {
        "id": "say_hello",
        "name": "Say hello",
        "function": { "name": "cli_command", "input": { "command": "echo", "args": ["hello"] } }
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

A command-driven step that reuses a prompt (from
`packages/system-author-story/1.0.0/definition.json`):

```json
{
  "id": "author_story_cursor",
  "name": "Author story with Cursor (auto mode)",
  "function": {
    "name": "command",
    "input": {
      "command_id": "cursor-agent",
      "config": {},
      "prompt": "{{prompt.system-story-authoring}}\n\n## Operator request\n{{runtime.story_request}}"
    }
  },
  "next_tasks": []
}
```

## Rules

- Declare every `{{runtime.KEY}}` under `runtime_inputs`.
- `{{userinput.TASK_ID.value}}` must reference a `user_input` task that runs earlier.
- Set `spoke_root` when `command` tasks run with cwd `spoke`.
- No secrets in workflow JSON.

The bundled `workflow` skill (installed with the workflow-authoring bundle)
carries the full task-handler and template-expansion reference.

## Schema reference

`see.library/v1` - see the [workflow schema](/schema/workflow) and the
[catalog schema](/schema/).

---

# Bundle schema

Source: content/schema/bundle.mdx

Category `bundle`. No payload of its own.

A bundle's catalog `files[]` array installs the payloads of several other
packages together; the build infers each file's kind from its `to` prefix (see
[Install paths](/schema/#install-paths)).

Bundle metadata lives in its `s_e_e_package.json`:

- `id` - bundle id (e.g. `bundle-author-story`).
- `slug` - url/file-safe slug.
- `category` - `bundle`.
- `name` - display name.
- `description` - one-line summary.
- `labels` - string array.
- `dependencies` - string array of the package `id`s it pulls in.

## JSON Schema

Schema for a bundle's `s_e_e_package.json`. Download:
[bundle-package.schema.json](https://garunski.github.io/s_e_e_library/schema/bundle-package.schema.json)

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "$id": "https://garunski.github.io/s_e_e_library/schema/bundle-package.schema.json",
  "title": "Bundle metadata (s_e_e_package.json)",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["id", "slug", "category", "name"],
  "properties": {
    "id": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
    "slug": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
    "category": { "const": "bundle" },
    "name": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
    "description": { "type": "string" },
    "labels": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 } },
    "dependencies": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 } }
  }
}
```

The bundle's `files[]` entries follow the `file` definition in the
[catalog schema](/schema/#json-schema).

See the [bundle authoring guide](/authoring/bundles) and the
[catalog schema](/schema/).

---

# Command schema

Source: content/schema/command.mdx

Category `command`. Payload file `command.json`. Installs to
`.s_e_e/commands/{id}.json` (the `to` path must equal this exactly).

- `id` - unique command id; also the install filename. Required, non-empty.
- `name`, `icon` - display metadata.
- `binary` - executable to run. Required, non-empty.
- `base_args` - args always passed.
- `forced_args` - args the runner appends; not operator-overridable.
- `workspace_arg` - `{ flag, value }` for passing the working directory.
- `prompt` - prompt delivery (e.g. `{ "delivery": "stdin" }`).
- `params[]` - typed parameters; each has `key`, `type`, optional `default`, and `map.target` (`env`, `timeout`, `args_append`, `lock_policy`, `stream_toggle`).
- `env` - static environment variables.
- `result` - `success_when` plus `capture[]`.
- `execution` - `cwd` (`spoke`/`hub`), `lock`, `stream`, `non_interactive`.

A payload with `content` but no `binary` is treated as a prompt.

## JSON Schema

Download: [command.schema.json](https://garunski.github.io/s_e_e_library/schema/command.schema.json)

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "$id": "https://garunski.github.io/s_e_e_library/schema/command.schema.json",
  "title": "Command package payload (command.json)",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["id", "binary"],
  "properties": {
    "id": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
    "name": { "type": "string" },
    "icon": { "type": "string" },
    "binary": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
    "base_args": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string" } },
    "forced_args": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string" } },
    "workspace_arg": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": { "flag": { "type": "string" }, "value": { "type": "string" } }
    },
    "prompt": { "type": "object", "properties": { "delivery": { "type": "string" } } },
    "params": { "type": "array", "items": { "$ref": "#/$defs/param" } },
    "env": { "type": "object", "additionalProperties": { "type": "string" } },
    "result": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "success_when": { "type": "string" },
        "capture": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string" } }
      }
    },
    "execution": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "cwd": { "enum": ["spoke", "hub"] },
        "lock": { "type": "object" },
        "stream": { "type": "object" },
        "non_interactive": { "type": "boolean" }
      }
    }
  },
  "$defs": {
    "param": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["key"],
      "properties": {
        "key": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
        "type": { "type": "string" },
        "default": {},
        "map": {
          "type": "object",
          "required": ["target"],
          "properties": {
            "target": { "enum": ["env", "timeout", "args_append", "lock_policy", "stream_toggle"] },
            "name": { "type": "string" }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

See the [command authoring guide](/authoring/commands) and the
[catalog schema](/schema/).

---

# Catalog schema

Source: content/schema/index.mdx

The catalog is a single JSON manifest (`catalog.json`) plus versioned payload
files under `packages/`. The manifest schema tag is `see.library/v1`. This
section is the authoritative contract for this repository;
`scripts/build-catalog.mjs` enforces it.

Per-type payload schemas:

- [Workflow](/schema/workflow)
- [Prompt](/schema/prompt)
- [Skill](/schema/skill)
- [Command](/schema/command)
- [Rule &amp; template](/schema/rule-template)
- [Bundle](/schema/bundle)

## Manifest

`catalog.json`:

- `schema` - must be `see.library/v1`.
- `name` - catalog display name.
- `updated` - ISO-8601 timestamp (refreshed by `npm run build`).
- `packages[]` - array of package entries.

## Package entry

Each entry in `packages[]`:

- `id` - string, globally unique within the catalog.
- `slug` - string, url/file-safe.
- `category` - one of `workflow`, `prompt`, `skill`, `rule`, `template`, `command`, `bundle`.
- `version` - semver string.
- `author` - string.
- `verified` - bool.
- `license` - SPDX id (e.g. `AGPL-3.0-only`).
- `requires` - string array of external tools that must be present (empty when none).
- `dependencies` - string array of other package `id`s installed alongside this one.
- `files[]` - install map; each entry is `{ to, from }`:
  - `from` - source path in this repo, `packages/{slug}/{version}/{file}`.
  - `to` - destination path written on the install target, relative to the target root.

## Package metadata sidecar

Every package carries a `packages/{slug}/s_e_e_package.json` (or
`s_e_e_package.{id}.json` when a slug has multiple entries); the build
cross-checks it against the catalog entry:

- `id` - package id; matches the catalog entry.
- `slug` - url/file-safe slug.
- `category` - one of the category values above.
- `name` - display name.
- `description` - one-line summary.
- `labels` - string array (the build dedupes and sorts it).
- `dependencies` - string array of other package `id`s.

## Categories

Each category has a fixed payload shape and install destination:

- `workflow` - a workflow definition JSON. Installs to `.s_e_e/workflows/definitions/{slug}.json`.
- `prompt` - a prompt JSON (`id`, `name`, `content`). Installs to `.s_e_e/prompts/{file}.json`.
- `skill` - a `SKILL.md` with `name` + `description` frontmatter. Installs to `.agents/skills/{slug}/SKILL.md`.
- `rule` - a Cursor rule `.mdc` file. Installs to `.cursor/rules/{slug}.mdc`.
- `template` - a scaffold file. Installs under `templates/`.
- `command` - a command definition JSON (`id`, `binary`). Installs to `.s_e_e/commands/{id}.json` (the `to` path must equal this exactly).
- `bundle` - no payload of its own; its `files[]` install several other packages together. The build infers each file's kind from its `to` path.

## Install paths

Every `files[]` `to` path must:

- start with one of `.s_e_e/`, `.agents/`, `.cursor/`, or `templates/`;
- use forward slashes;
- be unique within the package.

For a `bundle`, the `to` prefix determines the payload kind that is validated:

- `.s_e_e/workflows/definitions/…` → workflow
- `.s_e_e/prompts/…` → prompt
- `.s_e_e/commands/…` → command
- `.agents/skills/…/SKILL.md` → skill
- `*.mdc` → rule
- `templates/…` → template

## Validation

`dependencies` must reference package `id`s present in the catalog, and a
package cannot depend on itself. Run `npm run build` to validate payloads and
refresh the manifest, or `npm run validate` (`--check`, no writes) in CI.

## JSON Schema

Download: [catalog.schema.json](https://garunski.github.io/s_e_e_library/schema/catalog.schema.json)

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "$id": "https://garunski.github.io/s_e_e_library/schema/catalog.schema.json",
  "title": "S.E.E. Library catalog manifest",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["schema", "name", "packages"],
  "properties": {
    "schema": { "const": "see.library/v1" },
    "name": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
    "updated": { "type": "string", "format": "date-time" },
    "packages": {
      "type": "array",
      "items": { "$ref": "#/$defs/package" }
    }
  },
  "$defs": {
    "package": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": [
        "id", "slug", "category", "version", "author",
        "verified", "license", "requires", "dependencies", "files"
      ],
      "properties": {
        "id": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
        "slug": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
        "category": {
          "enum": ["workflow", "prompt", "skill", "rule", "template", "command", "bundle"]
        },
        "version": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
        "author": { "type": "string" },
        "verified": { "type": "boolean" },
        "license": { "type": "string" },
        "requires": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string" } },
        "dependencies": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 } },
        "name": { "type": "string" },
        "description": { "type": "string" },
        "labels": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 } },
        "files": { "type": "array", "minItems": 1, "items": { "$ref": "#/$defs/file" } }
      }
    },
    "file": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["to", "from"],
      "properties": {
        "to": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^(\\.s_e_e/|\\.agents/|\\.cursor/|templates/)[^\\\\]*$" },
        "from": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Package metadata sidecar - download:
[package-meta.schema.json](https://garunski.github.io/s_e_e_library/schema/package-meta.schema.json)

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "$id": "https://garunski.github.io/s_e_e_library/schema/package-meta.schema.json",
  "title": "Package metadata sidecar (s_e_e_package.json)",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["id", "slug", "category", "name"],
  "properties": {
    "id": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
    "slug": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
    "category": {
      "enum": ["workflow", "prompt", "skill", "rule", "template", "command", "bundle"]
    },
    "name": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
    "description": { "type": "string" },
    "labels": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 } },
    "dependencies": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 } }
  }
}
```

---

# Prompt schema

Source: content/schema/prompt.mdx

Category `prompt`. Payload file `prompt.json`. Installs to
`.s_e_e/prompts/{file}.json`.

- `id` - prompt id used by `{{prompt.ID}}`. Required, non-empty; may carry a namespace prefix.
- `name` - display name. Required, non-empty.
- `content` - prompt body; a single string (use `\n` for line breaks).
- `created_at` - ISO-8601 timestamp. Optional.

A payload that contains `tasks` is treated as a workflow, not a prompt.

## JSON Schema

Download: [prompt.schema.json](https://garunski.github.io/s_e_e_library/schema/prompt.schema.json)

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "$id": "https://garunski.github.io/s_e_e_library/schema/prompt.schema.json",
  "title": "Prompt package payload (prompt.json)",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["id", "name"],
  "not": { "required": ["tasks"] },
  "properties": {
    "id": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
    "name": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
    "content": { "type": "string" },
    "created_at": { "type": "string", "format": "date-time" }
  }
}
```

See the [prompt authoring guide](/authoring/prompts) and the
[catalog schema](/schema/).

---

# Rule &amp; template schema

Source: content/schema/rule-template.mdx

Two lightweight, file-only categories.

## Rule

Category `rule`. Payload is a Cursor rule `.mdc` file (markdown with rule
frontmatter). Installs to `.cursor/rules/{slug}.mdc`.

## Template

Category `template`. Payload is an arbitrary scaffold file. Installs under
`templates/`.

See the [catalog schema](/schema/) for the manifest and install-path rules.

---

# Skill schema

Source: content/schema/skill.mdx

Category `skill`. Payload file `SKILL.md`. Installs to
`.agents/skills/{slug}/SKILL.md`.

`SKILL.md` is YAML frontmatter plus a markdown body. Frontmatter:

- `name` - non-empty skill name.
- `description` - non-empty activation description an agent matches against.

The markdown after the frontmatter is the skill instructions.

## JSON Schema

The payload is a markdown file; this schema describes its YAML frontmatter
block. Download: [skill-frontmatter.schema.json](https://garunski.github.io/s_e_e_library/schema/skill-frontmatter.schema.json)

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "$id": "https://garunski.github.io/s_e_e_library/schema/skill-frontmatter.schema.json",
  "title": "Skill SKILL.md YAML frontmatter",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["name", "description"],
  "properties": {
    "name": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
    "description": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 }
  }
}
```

See the [skill authoring guide](/authoring/skills) and the
[catalog schema](/schema/).

---

# Workflow schema

Source: content/schema/workflow.mdx

Category `workflow`. Payload file `definition.json`. Installs to
`.s_e_e/workflows/definitions/{slug}.json`.

An outer envelope wraps the engine `content`:

- `id` - `system-<name>` (defaults) or `user-<name>`. Required.
- `name`, `description`, `version` - strings.
- `content` - the engine workflow object:
  - `id` - required, non-empty.
  - `name` - required.
  - `tasks[]` - required, non-empty.
  - `runtime_inputs[]` - optional; each `{ key, input_type, required, label }`; `key` must not contain dots.
  - `spoke_root`, `hub_root`, `stories_root`, `knowledge_root` - optional root paths.

## Tasks

Each task:

- `id` - unique within the workflow.
- `name` - string.
- `function` - exactly one handler key: `cli_command`, `command`, `user_input`, `git_action`, `story_action`, `foreach`, or `custom`.
- `next_tasks[]` - optional nested tasks (depth-first execution).
- `capture_output` - when true, record handler output for `{{task.<id>.output...}}`.
- `wait_for` - task ids that must complete before this task runs (use a foreach id to wait for all iterations).
- `continue_on_failure` - when true, a failed task completes without failing the workflow; output is still captured when `capture_output` is true.
- `run_when` - object `{ task, path?, op, value? }` gating this task on a prior task's captured output (`op`: `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `lt`, `ge`, `le`, `exists`).

A payload containing `tasks` is treated as a workflow (not a prompt).

## Template expansion

- `{{prompt.ID}}` - injects a stored prompt; the id must exist in the hub.
- `{{runtime.KEY}}` - a run-start value; declared in `runtime_inputs`, no dots.
- `{{userinput.TASK_ID.value}}` - a value from an earlier `user_input` task.
- `{{task.TASK_ID.output...}}` - captured output from an earlier task with `capture_output: true` (`cli_command` output is `{ exit_code, stdout, stderr }`).

## JSON Schema

Download: [workflow.schema.json](https://garunski.github.io/s_e_e_library/schema/workflow.schema.json)

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "$id": "https://garunski.github.io/s_e_e_library/schema/workflow.schema.json",
  "title": "Workflow package payload (definition.json)",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["id", "name", "content"],
  "properties": {
    "id": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
    "name": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
    "description": { "type": "string" },
    "version": { "type": "string" },
    "content": {
      "oneOf": [
        { "type": "string", "description": "JSON-encoded workflow object" },
        { "$ref": "#/$defs/workflow" }
      ]
    }
  },
  "$defs": {
    "workflow": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["id", "name", "tasks"],
      "properties": {
        "id": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
        "name": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
        "spoke_root": { "type": "string" },
        "hub_root": { "type": "string" },
        "stories_root": { "type": "string" },
        "knowledge_root": { "type": "string" },
        "runtime_inputs": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": { "$ref": "#/$defs/runtime_input" }
        },
        "tasks": {
          "type": "array",
          "minItems": 1,
          "items": { "$ref": "#/$defs/task" }
        }
      }
    },
    "runtime_input": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["key", "input_type"],
      "properties": {
        "key": {
          "type": "string",
          "pattern": "^[^.]+$",
          "description": "Referenced as {{runtime.KEY}}; no dots."
        },
        "input_type": { "type": "string" },
        "required": { "type": "boolean" },
        "label": { "type": "string" }
      }
    },
    "task": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["id", "name", "function"],
      "properties": {
        "id": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
        "name": { "type": "string" },
        "function": { "$ref": "#/$defs/function" },
        "next_tasks": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": { "$ref": "#/$defs/task" }
        },
        "capture_output": {
          "type": "boolean",
          "default": false,
          "description": "Record handler output for `{{task.<id>.output...}}` templates"
        },
        "wait_for": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
          "description": "Task ids that must complete before this task runs"
        },
        "continue_on_failure": {
          "type": "boolean",
          "default": false,
          "description": "Treat a failed task as complete so descendants and captured output stay reachable"
        },
        "run_when": {
          "$ref": "#/$defs/condition",
          "description": "Gate execution on a prior task's captured output"
        }
      }
    },
    "condition": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["task", "op"],
      "properties": {
        "task": {
          "type": "string",
          "minLength": 1,
          "description": "Producer task id (capture_output: true)"
        },
        "path": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },
          "default": [],
          "description": "Path into producer output (e.g. exit_code, stdout)"
        },
        "op": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": ["eq", "ne", "gt", "lt", "ge", "le", "exists"]
        },
        "value": {
          "description": "Comparison value; omit when op is exists"
        }
      }
    },
    "function": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["name"],
      "properties": {
        "name": {
          "enum": [
            "cli_command",
            "command",
            "user_input",
            "git_action",
            "story_action",
            "foreach",
            "custom"
          ]
        },
        "input": { "type": "object" }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

See the [workflow authoring guide](/authoring/workflows) and the
[catalog schema](/schema/).
